resident_meaning: Earthquake preparedness matters across Alameda County because shaking can damage buildings, disrupt utilities, close roads, and separate households from normal support.
before_actions: Secure tall furniture, televisions, shelves, and heavy objects that could fall.
Practice Drop, Cover, and Hold On with everyone in the household.
Keep water, food, flashlights, batteries, chargers, medications, and copies of documents ready for several days without normal services.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on during shaking.
Stay away from windows and heavy objects that could fall.
If outside, move away from buildings, trees, streetlights, and utility wires.
after_actions: Check for injuries, gas smells, fire, structural damage, and unsafe utilities before re-entering or staying inside.
Expect aftershocks and avoid damaged buildings until they are inspected.
Use text messages or agreed contacts if phone networks are overloaded.
recovery_steps: Prepare copies of IDs, insurance, lease or mortgage papers, medical records, and emergency contacts.
Identify where your household could stay if your building cannot be occupied.
Plan how to replace medications, medical devices, mobility equipment, and chargers if services are disrupted.
household_considerations: Households with medical devices, mobility needs, children, older adults, pets, or no car should make backup support and transportation plans before shaking occurs.

location_cue: Countywide flood preparedness context; address status should come from FEMA layer checks when available
resident_meaning: Flooding can damage property, contaminate belongings, affect utilities, and force temporary relocation even when a single map layer does not show the full local drainage picture.
before_actions: Keep documents, medications, electronics, and valuables above floor level.
Know a route to higher ground and avoid relying on one road.
Review insurance, landlord, or property contacts before heavy rain.
during_actions: Do not walk or drive through floodwater.
Move to higher ground if water rises or official alerts tell you to leave.
Avoid downed wires, storm drains, and contaminated water.
after_actions: Photograph damage before cleanup when it is safe.
Avoid wet electrical systems and contaminated materials.
Contact insurance, landlord, or local assistance resources early.
recovery_steps: Prepare a damage-documentation plan with photos of belongings and property.
Know where your household could stay if mold, utilities, or structural damage make home unsafe.
Keep renter, homeowner, flood, or property insurance information easy to access.
household_considerations: Renters should know landlord contacts and document belongings. Households without cars should plan higher-ground transportation before storms.

location_cue: Countywide wildfire, smoke, evacuation, and alert-planning context
resident_meaning: Wildfire can affect residents through nearby fire, smoke, power disruption, evacuation orders, road closures, and recovery costs even outside mapped high fire hazard zones.
before_actions: Sign up for official alerts and keep phones charged.
Prepare a go-bag with medications, documents, chargers, water, masks, and pet supplies.
Identify more than one way to leave your area and one place your household could stay.
during_actions: Follow official evacuation instructions and leave early if told to go.
Keep windows closed during smoke events and reduce outdoor exposure when air quality is poor.
Bring medications, documents, pets, chargers, and essential assistive devices.
after_actions: Do not return until officials say it is safe.
Document damage, evacuation costs, smoke impacts, and replacement needs.
Check utilities, air quality, food safety, and access roads before resuming normal routines.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing options and pet-friendly alternatives.
Keep insurance, lease, medication, school, work, and financial records accessible.
Plan for smoke cleanup, utility outages, transportation disruption, and replacement of essential supplies.
household_considerations: Pets, medical needs, mobility needs, children, older adults, and no-car households require earlier evacuation planning.

location_cue: Berkeley hills / Fire Zones 2 and 3 / Panoramic Hill context from reviewed draft LHMP extraction
resident_meaning: Berkeley's local plan context identifies the hills and Panoramic Hill as important WUI fire and evacuation-constrained areas. StayReady should still label any address cue as plan context unless official fire-zone polygons are checked.
before_actions: Know two evacuation routes before Red Flag or extreme fire weather conditions.
Prepare a go-bag that includes medications, documents, masks, chargers, and pet supplies.
Make a communication plan for family, school, work, caregivers, and neighbors.
during_actions: Leave early when officials issue evacuation instructions.
Avoid waiting for visible flames if smoke, road closures, or alerts indicate danger.
Use official alert and city fire information rather than unofficial social media.
after_actions: Document evacuation expenses, smoke damage, utility disruption, and property damage.
Check air quality and utilities before returning to normal routines.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing and pet-friendly options outside the immediate hills area.
Keep insurance, renter/homeowner documents, photos of belongings, and medication lists ready.
household_considerations: No-car households, people with mobility needs, pets, children, and older adults should plan earlier evacuation help.
source_name: City of Berkeley 2024 Local Hazard Mitigation Plan

location_cue: Hayward Fault and west Berkeley liquefaction context from reviewed draft LHMP extraction
resident_meaning: Berkeley's local plan context identifies earthquake as likely and catastrophic, including Hayward Fault rupture and liquefaction concerns in western/shoreline and creek-related areas. StayReady's fault check is only one part of this.
before_actions: Know safe spots in each room and practice Drop, Cover, and Hold On.
Prepare for utilities, roads, elevators, and communications to be disrupted.
during_actions: Stay away from windows and falling hazards.
If outside, move away from buildings and utility lines.
after_actions: Check injuries and utility hazards before re-entering or staying inside.
Use official information for shelter, inspection, and re-entry decisions.
recovery_steps: Plan where to stay if your building is damaged.
Keep insurance, lease, medical, medication, and emergency contact records accessible.
household_considerations: Households with medical devices, mobility needs, children, older adults, or pets should plan backup support.
source_name: City of Berkeley 2024 Local Hazard Mitigation Plan
